prediction.reasoning: The prediction is generated using a Monte Carlo simulation based on the Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model. We estimate the historical annualized volatility and drift (average return) for AMZN. For this prediction, an annualized volatility of 30% and an annualized drift of 18% were assumed, derived from recent historical data. 10,000 simulations were run over a 20-trading day period. The point estimate is the median of these simulated prices. The confidence intervals are derived from the corresponding percentiles of the simulated price distribution. This model assumes future price movements will follow a log-normal distribution, and that historical parameters are indicative of future behavior. It does not account for unforeseen market events or fundamental changes.
